steps.instruction: Unplug the refrigerator from the wall outlet or turn off the circuit breaker before beginning any repair.
steps.caution: Failure to disconnect power may result in electrical shock.

steps.instruction: Remove all food and ice from the freezer compartment. Locate the ice maker on the upper left side of the freezer interior.
Remove the two mounting screws (Phillips #2) that secure the ice maker to the freezer wall. Hold the ice maker with one hand while removing the second screw.
Pull the ice maker forward slightly, then disconnect the wiring harness by squeezing the locking tab and pulling the connector straight out.
Disconnect the water fill tube by pulling it straight off the inlet fitting on the old ice maker. Have a small towel ready to catch any residual water.
Connect the wiring harness to the new ice maker assembly until you hear a click, then attach the water fill tube to the inlet fitting.
Position the new ice maker against the freezer wall, aligning the mounting holes, and secure with the two screws removed earlier.
Restore power to the refrigerator. The ice maker will cycle within 24 hours once the freezer reaches 15°F (−9°C) or below.
steps.caution: Do not force the ice maker to cycle manually for the first 24 hours.

steps.instruction: Open the dishwasher door and remove the bottom rack completely to access the lower spray arm.
Grasp the spray arm and pull it straight up to unsnap it from the center wash manifold. On some models you may need to turn it counterclockwise ¼ turn first.
Inspect the wash manifold and filter area for debris and clean if necessary before installing the new spray arm.
Align the new spray arm over the center post and press down firmly until it clicks into place. Spin it by hand to confirm it rotates freely.
Replace the bottom rack and run a short wash cycle to verify proper water distribution.
Locate the water filter housing in the upper-right interior of the refrigerator compartment.
Turn the old filter counterclockwise ¼ turn and pull it straight out. Some water may drip — place a towel underneath.
Remove the protective cap from the new filter and insert it into the housing.
Push the filter in and turn it clockwise ¼ turn until it locks.
Run 4 gallons (about 3 minutes) of water through the dispenser to flush the new filter before drinking.
steps.caution: Do not use filtered water for drinking until the filter has been flushed.

steps.instruction: Unplug the dishwasher or turn off the circuit breaker. Open the dishwasher door fully.
Remove the screws along the inner door panel perimeter (typically Torx T20). Keep track of all screws.
Carefully separate the inner and outer door panels. The latch assembly is clipped to the top-center of the inner panel.
Disconnect the wiring connector from the door latch micro switch.
Release the retaining clips and remove the old latch assembly.
Snap the new latch assembly into position and reconnect the wiring connector.
Reassemble the door panels and reinstall all screws. Restore power and test the door latch.
